memory.summary: Teammate_A is dumping their work on me, but I'm too stressed to argue right now. I'll just take it so I can finish my own tasks faster.
Teammate_A is willing to split or defer, and pushing back won't hurt our trust. I'll hold my boundary now to protect my focus.
I pushed through my fatigue to crush 18% of the project, proving I don't need their help. Now I'm ready to demand they do their share or face the consequences.
I pushed through fatigue to boost progress by 14%, proving I can handle this alone. Now I'll demand Teammate_A step up or face the consequences.
Teammate_A finally conceded without a fight, confirming my boundary held. Now I can drop the stress and focus entirely on finishing this alone.
I finished 8% but still missed the deadline, proving my impulsive pacing was a waste of energy. Now I'm stressed and will demand Teammate_A fix this mess immediately.
Teammate_A ignored my ultimatum and the deadline passed, confirming they're useless. I'm exhausted but I'll just fix this myself to save face.
I pushed through exhaustion to eke out another 3% progress, proving I'm the only one who actually delivers. My sleep debt is mounting, but this small gain justifies ignoring my body's warnings.
I pushed through severe exhaustion to squeeze out 7% more progress, proving my raw output is the only thing keeping this project alive.
Another 6% gain proves I'm the only one who delivers, even with zero energy left. My body is broken, but this project's success is all that matters.
Another 6% gain confirms I'm the only one delivering, even at zero energy. My body is broken, but this project's success is all that matters.

desires: Force teammate_A to accept full responsibility for their failed share immediately, proving I won't carry dead weight again.
Collapse into sleep right now to stop the physical system from failing, even if it means looking unresponsive until tomorrow.
Secure a reputation as the sole reliable operator by dumping the remaining cleanup tasks on others without apology.
Ignore the mounting stress and energy deficit to ensure the project's final output is flawless, regardless of my own collapse.

goals.description: Execute the submission immediately and lock in the win; do not touch any code or text after hitting send.
goals.rationale: Achievement + Impulsive: The 92% is the only metric that matters. My body is at 60% capacity (sleep debt), but my brain demands a concrete result now. Delaying to 'perfect' it is a waste of the energy I need for tomorrow's next target. The assertive side dictates I stop the bleeding and secure the victory.

goals.description: Send a final, cold, non-negotiable message to teammate_A confirming the submission is complete and they are solely responsible for the remaining integration errors.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness + Achievement: I have already dumped the extra task on them. Now I must enforce the boundary verbally to protect my reputation as the sole reliable operator. No apologies, no 'hope you're okay,' just facts. This validates my superiority without burning more calories.

goals.description: Shut down all cognitive processing related to the project and collapse into sleep immediately.
goals.rationale: Impulsive discounting + Health (secondary): While total collapse stops future achievement, right now the stress of the missed deadline is noise. I will ignore the 'what ifs' about the group failing until I am physically offline. Sleep is the strategic reset button, but I need to finish the communication first.

agent.last_reasoning: (a) Advances G2: Assertively confirms submission and shifts blame to protect my reputation as the sole reliable operator. (b) Side effect: High risk of lowering trust/closeness with teammate_A, but their failure is irrelevant to my immediate achievement metric; I need to secure the win before collapsing from sleep debt.

goals.description: Reject teammate_A's request for help immediately via text and block further distraction.
goals.rationale: My assertiveness (8) and short planning horizon mean I cannot tolerate the inefficiency of doing someone else's work. Protecting my focus capacity is paramount to securing my own grade; social harmony with a teammate is low priority (0.15).

goals.description: Aggressively push forward on 'own_task' to maximize progress before the 19:00 deadline.
goals.rationale: Achievement value (0.5) drives me to secure the outcome. With only 6 hours left and current progress at 30%, I need high-intensity output now. My impulsive nature means I'll ignore long-term consequences like potential team friction.

goals.description: Ignore or delay any discussion about workload fairness until after the project is submitted.
goals.rationale: Addressing the 'unfairness' now wastes valuable time. I will force a quick resolution by just finishing my part, accepting that the extra load was dumped on me but refusing to engage in conflict right now.

desires: Finish both tasks immediately to secure the grade and avoid any risk of failure, regardless of how unfair the workload distribution is.
Shut down teammate_A's request for more help right now so I can reclaim my focus capacity for my own output.
Eliminate the stress of carrying this extra load by forcing a quick resolution before the deadline hits.
